sentenceFrame: The bell is on the hill.
celebration: Leaf #4 — the HARVEST leaf. The biggest single week of Level 2 (+40 words). ck is formalized (known since Week 21) and the FLoSs doubling rule unlocks a whole family of words that end in a doubled f, l, s or z.

songs.title: Ck! Ck! Bell!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
Ck! Ck! Ck-ck-ck!
(k… k… k…)

[Hook]
Ck says /k/ at the end!
Sock! Rock! Duck! Kick!
Ck-ck… /k/! /k/!
Two letters, ONE sound!

[Verse 2]
F, L, S, Z — hear the rule!
A little word? DOUBLE it!
Be-ll! BELL! Pu-ff! PUFF!
Two at the end! (double! double!)

[Verse 3]
Mi-ss! MISS! O-ff! OFF!
Fi-zz! Hi-ss! Ye-ll!
Double the L! Double the S!
Two at the end! (double! double!)

[Verse 4]
Bell! Bell! On the hill!
Up the hill — puff, puff, puff!
Ck-ck… /k/! (sock! rock!)
Two letters, ONE sound!

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! No double! Not ck!
(k… k… k…)

[Outro]
Bell! Bell! On the hill!
Ck-ck… /k/! /k/!
(hush… hush… hush…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ck is a single /k/ click at the END of a word — never 'see-kay'. The doubling verses teach the FLoSs rule: a short one-syllable word ending in f, l, s or z DOUBLES that last letter (bell, puff, miss, fizz). Chant each double as a beat-doubled 808 knock ('be-LL' = two hits landing as one word). The whole lesson is that double letters make ONE sound — exactly like ck and the digraphs. Never sing letter names anywhere.

songs.title: The Bell on the Hill
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The bell is on the hill!
The bell is on the hill!
Where is the bell? (where? where?)
The bell is on the hill!

[Verse 2]
Chick can hit the bell!
Up! Up! Up the hill!
Puff, puff, up the hill! (puff! puff!)
The bell is on the hill!

[Verse 3]
Can Sheep miss the bell?
No! Sheep will not miss!
Hit it! Hit it! (bell! bell!)
The bell fell off the hill!

[Outro]
The bell… the hill… (hush…)
A potato?! (POTATO!)
Off the hill?! (roll, potato, roll!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The bell is on the hill.' Deliberately ng-free — 'ring' and 'ding' arrive next week (Week 31, ng). Verse 2 lands 'puff' and 'off'; verse 3 lands 'miss', 'will' and 'fell'. Every core word appears. Whisper the outro, potato hits loud, then rolls away.

spreads.artNote: A single grassy hill rising into the spotlight against the dark, one lonely tree at the top. Room for a climb.
A big brass hand-bell sitting at the very top of the hill, gleaming, begging to be rung. The tree leans over it.
Chick at the bottom of the hill, eyes locked on the distant bell with total determination, one wing pointing up. The quest begins.
Chick struggling gamely halfway up the slope, tiny puffs of effort around her beak, legs a blur. Comedy of a very small climber.
Triumph — Chick at the summit, headbutting the big bell, a burst of shine lines around the impact. Pure joy.
Uh-oh — the bell has tipped off its post and is tumbling, Chick's eyes going wide, wings thrown up in alarm.

spreads.text: The bell is off the hill.
spreads.artNote: The bell rolling downhill in a blur, Chick chasing after it, a comic dust trail behind them both.
Sheep — a fluffy round white sheep with kind dark eyes — waiting patiently at the bottom, catching the runaway bell neatly on its back. Rescue complete. Chick cheers.
Where the bell used to sit at the top of the hill, a single potato now rests in the spotlight. Chick and Sheep stare up at it. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

sentences: The bell is on the hill.
extras: PATTERN TREE: leaf #4 — the harvest leaf, the biggest single week of Level 2 (+40 words). Read bell/miss/off/puff aloud and hang it.

THE FLoSs RULE presentation: a short one-syllable word ending in f, l, s or z DOUBLES that last letter — off, bell, miss, buzz. Build 'be' + 'l' and show it looks unfinished; add the second l and it 'clicks'. The double makes ONE sound.
ck FORMALIZED: the class has read ck since Week 21 ('same /k/, two letters'); today it joins the doubling family officially — sock, rock, duck, kick.
EXCEPTION BASKET (oral): bull, full, pull — these end in double-l but the u says /ʊ/, not the short /ʌ/. Taught by ear as friendly exceptions, never sounded out.
Moveable alphabet with a spare l, s, f, z, and a ck tile — build bell, miss, off, puff, sock.

teacherFocus: THE HARVEST WEEK — the biggest of Level 2 (+40 words). Two things: ck (finally formalized — the class has read it since Week 21 as 'same /k/, two letters', so today it just gets its name) and the FLoSs doubling rule (a short one-syllable word ending in f, l, s or z doubles that last letter: off, bell, miss, buzz). Present the doubling with the moveable alphabet: build 'be-l' and let the child feel it looks unfinished, then add the twin l and it 'clicks' — the double makes ONE sound, the same lesson as sh/ch/th/ck. Keep the exception basket (bull, full, pull) oral only — those u's say /ʊ/ and must be learned by ear, never sounded out. Hang the harvest leaf with real ceremony.
